file_name: ADMA BioManufacturing, LLC -  Amendment #3 to Manufacturing Agreement .txt

perturbation.original_text: Sanofi Pasteur agrees to purchase and ADMA to manufacture [***] Batches of Product, which Batches will be produced over a period from Q3 2018 to Q3 2019. Attached hereto as Exhibit A is a detailed supply plan provided to ADMA by Sanofi Pasteur (the "Updated Supply Plan") that describes the agreed-upon timing for production of such Batches of Product, which supply plan is made an integral part hereof and shall be binding on the Parties. Prices for such [***] Batches of Product shall be in accordance with Section 3.2 of the Agreement (as amended in Amendment #2).
perturbation.changed_text: Sanofi Pasteur agrees to purchase and ADMA to manufacture [***] Units of Deliverable, which Units of Deliverable will be produced over a period from Q3 2018 to Q3 2019. Attached hereto as Exhibit A is a detailed supply plan provided to ADMA by Sanofi Pasteur (the "Updated Supply Plan") that describes the agreed-upon timing for production of such Batches of Product, which supply plan is made an integral part hereof and shall be binding on the Parties. Prices for such [***] Batches of Product shall be in accordance with Section 3.2 of the Agreement (as amended in Amendment #2).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Batches of Product' to 'Units of Deliverable' creates confusion because Exhibit A and subsequent references still use 'Batches of Product'. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear if the supply plan refers to 'Units of Deliverable' or 'Batches of Product,' and whether they are the same. This is especially problematic because the supply plan defines the timing and quantity obligations. The term 'Batches of Product' is now contradicted within the same paragraph, as the attached exhibit describes the timing for 'Batches of Product'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sanofi Pasteur agrees to purchase and ADMA to manufacture [***] Units of Deliverable, which Units of Deliverable will be produced over a period from Q3 2018 to Q3 2019. Attached hereto as Exhibit A is a detailed supply plan provided to ADMA by Sanofi Pasteur (the "Updated Supply Plan") that describes the agreed-upon timing for production of such Batches of Product, which supply plan is made an integral part hereof and shall be binding on the Parties. Prices for such [***] Batches of Product shall be in accordance with Section 3.2 of the Agreement (as amended in Amendment #2). The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Batches of Product' to 'Units of Deliverable' creates a direct contradiction because Exhibit A and subsequent references, including the liquidated damages clause, still use 'Batches of Product'. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear if the supply plan refers to 'Units of Deliverable' or 'Batches of Product,' and whether they are the same. The contradiction arises because the modified text now refers to 'Units of Deliverable' while the referenced exhibit, which defines the crucial supply schedule, still uses 'Batches of Product'. This discrepancy leads to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, aligning with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Specifically, the contradicted text states: 'Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product...ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages...' This section is now directly contradicted by the introduction of 'Units of Deliverable' as the primary measure of product, creating ambiguity regarding whether the liquidated damages apply to failures related to 'Units of Deliverable' or only 'Batches of Product', or how the two terms reconcile.

perturbation.original_text: Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma.
perturbation.changed_text: Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Units of Deliverables (the "Minimum Volume") of Deliverables during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Batches of Product' to 'Units of Deliverables' while retaining 'Batches of Product' in later references creates an in-text contradiction. The paragraph now refers to liquidated damages for failing to supply 'Units of Deliverables', while the rest of Section 2 and Exhibit A still focus on 'Batches of Product'. This makes it unclear whether failure to supply 'Batches of Product' as per Exhibit A triggers the liquidated damages clause, since it now speaks of 'Units of Deliverables' as the trigger.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Units of Deliverables (the "Minimum Volume") of Deliverables during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Batches of Product' with 'Units of Deliverables' while the contradicted text still refers to 'Batches of Product'. This introduces a direct conflict and misalignment of terminology. The liquidated damages clause is now triggered by a failure to supply 'Units of Deliverables,' while other parts of the contract, including Exhibit A, focus on 'Batches of Product.' This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding whether failure to supply 'Batches of Product' triggers the liquidated damages clause, as the contradicted text clearly states obligations related to 'Batches of Product' as per Exhibit A. This directly follows the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to the Minimum Volume of Deliverables to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Volume of Product' to 'Volume of Deliverables', but maintaining 'Volume of Product' in the same sentence creates an in-text contradiction within the clause. This misaligned terminology makes it ambiguous which the Updated Supply Plan refers to, and creates uncertainty about what triggers the specific penalty for failing to meet the Updated Supply Plan. The contradiction is contained within this sentence, highlighting how easily misaligned terms can cause confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to the Minimum Volume of Deliverables to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty. The foregoing liquidated damages [***] respect to the [***] within the [***] agreed in this Amendment #3. [***] not be entitled to [***] by this Agreement as a result of [***], including without limitation [***]. Notwithstanding the foregoing, [***], sections 6.1 and 6.2 of the Agreement [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Volume of Deliverables' while retaining 'Volume of Product' in the same sentence. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the 'Updated Supply Plan' refers to 'Deliverables' or 'Product,' leading to ambiguity about which quantity is relevant for determining penalties, as the contradiced text also contains this error. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
